title: Garlicky Canadian Salmon Chowder
description: A hearty, midnight-craving salmon chowder with a garlicky kick and a splash of cayenne pepper for warmth.
instructions: Heat the olive oil and butter in a large pot over medium heat until the butter foams.
Add the diced onion, carrots, and celery; sauté for 5-7 minutes until the vegetables soften.
Stir in the minced garlic and cook for an additional 1 minute until fragrant, being careful not to burn it.
Pour in the fish stock, bring the mixture to a gentle boil, then reduce heat and simmer for 10 minutes to meld the flavors.
Add the cubed potatoes and continue to simmer until they are tender, about 8 minutes.
Fold in the salmon cubes and let the chowder gently simmer for 4-5 minutes, just until the salmon is opaque and cooked through—do not overcook to keep it juicy.
Stir in the heavy cream, cayenne pepper, smoked paprika, salt, and pepper; adjust seasonings to taste.
Add the chopped dill and a squeeze of lemon juice if using; let the chowder heat through for an extra 2 minutes.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with extra dill or a sprinkle of cayenne for a touch of heat.
Enjoy the garlicky, creamy, Canadian-inspired salmon chowder perfect for late-night cravings.
